sourceMovie.title: Inland Empire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Inland Empire (2006) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Lynch's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: After.Life
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Inland Empire clicked for you, After.Life (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Midsommar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Inland Empire clicked for you, Midsommar (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Lost Highway
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Inland Empire clicked for you, Lost Highway (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Inland Empire?
faq.answer: Try After.Life, Midsommar, Lost Highway—each shares DNA with Inland Empire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Inland Empire?
faq.answer: Inland Empire (2006) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Mystery, Drama, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Inland Empire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
